title: CIBC's privacy practices failed in cases of misdirected faxes
summary: In late 2004, the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada commenced an investigation into incidents involving misdirected facsimiles. These faxes, containing the personal information of CIBC customers, were sent by various branches of the bank to a company in the United States and another in Dorval, Quebec.
